file_name: NETGEAR,INC_04_21_2003-EX-10.16-AMENDMENT TO THE DISTRIBUTOR AGREEMENT BETWEEN INGRAM MICRO AND NETGEAR.txt

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either party without prior written permission from the other party, which permission shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Any attempt by either party to assign any right, or delegate any duty or obligation which arises under the Agreement without such permission will be voidable.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either party without prior written permission from the other party, which permission shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Any attempt by either side to transfer any right, or delegate any duty or obligation which arises under the Agreement without such permission will be void.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'party' to 'side' creates ambiguity. While 'party' clearly refers to the two entities involved (NETGEAR and Distributor), 'side' is less precise. It could be interpreted as a division within a single party, potentially leading to confusion about which entity needs permission for assignment. It contradicts the subsequent clause by creating ambiguity in the term 'party' in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either party without prior written permission from the other party, which permission shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Any attempt by either side to transfer any right, or delegate any duty or obligation which arises under the Agreement without such permission will be void.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'party' to 'side' introduces ambiguity, as 'side' is less precise and could be interpreted as a division within a single party, conflicting with the established meaning of 'party' in the agreement, specifically as it is defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The contradiction lies in the fact that 'side' is not a clearly defined term like 'party' in this context, creating uncertainty about who needs permission for assignment.

perturbation.original_text: The terms and conditions of this Amendment, shall amend and supersede any conflicting terms of the original Agreement. All other terms of the original Agreement shall remain unchanged.
perturbation.changed_text: The provisions and stipulations of this Amendment, shall amend and supersede any conflicting terms of the original Agreement. All other terms of the original Agreement shall remain unchanged.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "terms and conditions" to "provisions and stipulations" introduces a subtle but significant ambiguity. While these phrases are often used interchangeably, legally, "terms and conditions" encompasses the entire scope of the agreement's enforceable elements. "Provisions and stipulations," in contrast, might be interpreted more narrowly as specific clauses or requirements, potentially excluding other unstated or implied obligations or practices. This could lead to disputes over whether a particular element of the original agreement is superseded, especially if it's argued that the element falls outside the scope of "provisions and stipulations." The contradiction lies in whether the "All other terms of the original Agreement shall remain unchanged" statement remains valid with the new, ambiguous definition of what the 'amended' agreement covers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The provisions and stipulations of this Amendment, shall amend and supersede any conflicting terms of the original Agreement. All other terms of the original Agreement shall remain unchanged.
perturbation.justification: Although the explanation suggests a possible ambiguity created by changing "terms and conditions" to "provisions and stipulations," the provided text snippet for both the changed text and contradicted text is identical. There is no explicit or direct contradiction within the snippet itself. While the explanation suggests a potential for future disagreement, the language itself does not create a strong, immediate in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor agrees that the foregoing Discount is only applicable to Products included on the NETGEAR Price List on the Effective Date of this Agreement. NETGEAR reserves the right to add Products to the Price List at its sole discretion and any such additional Products shall be offered to Distributor at discounts to be determined at that time.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor agrees that the foregoing Discount is only applicable to Items included on the NETGEAR Price List on the Effective Date of this Agreement. NETGEAR reserves the right to add Products to the Price List at its sole discretion and any such additional Products shall be offered to Distributor at discounts to be determined at that time.
perturbation.explanation: The alteration from 'Products' to 'Items' introduces confusion regarding the scope of the discount. The contract references 'Products' consistently elsewhere when detailing what NETGEAR provides for distribution. Changing 'Products' to 'Items' creates ambiguity as to whether the agreed discount applies only to a subset of products (i.e., 'Items'), potentially excluding other product categories that were intended to be included. This could affect the application of the discount rates for certain types of goods, conflicting with the overall intent of the pricing structure. The contradiction here is the implied limitation that 'Items' introduces when the rest of the agreement still defines the scope of the agreement in relation to 'Products'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The initial Discount offered Distributor for purchase or license of NETGEAR Products included on the NETGEAR Price List in effect on the Effective Date of this Agreement is [*] off of the then current NETGEAR list price. Distributor agrees that the foregoing Discount is only applicable to Items included on the NETGEAR Price List on the Effective Date of this Agreement. NETGEAR reserves the right to add Products to the Price List at its sole discretion and any such additional Products shall be offered to Distributor at discounts to be determined at that time.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Products' to 'Items' introduces ambiguity regarding the scope of the discount. The contradicted text references the 'Discount offered Distributor for purchase or license of NETGEAR Products'. The changed text limits this to 'Items included on the NETGEAR Price List'. As the explanation notes, the rest of the agreement still defines the scope in relation to 'Products.' Therefore, the change creates a direct conflict within the document regarding which goods are covered by the discount, leading to a contradiction as outlined by the Misaligned Terminology definition.
